subject: FW: WEIZMANN INSTITUTE
body:
From: egneurocog@aol.com [mailto:egneurocog@aol.com] 
Sent: Monday, March 17, 2008 7:46 PM
To: Lesley Groff; Lesley Groff
Subject: WEIZMANN INSTITUTE

 

Dear Jeffrey,

 

I've given further thought to our discussion last week and to your
suggestion that people with more basic mathematical, as opposed to
engineering, background be brought into the project. Instead of thinking
in terms of individuals, as was my initial inclination, I approached the
issue more systemically. In my opinion, the absolutely best neural-net
modelling, and generally mathematical neurobiology talent is found in
Israel. Over the last few days I had an email exchange, and yesterday a
long telephone conversation with Prof. Misha Tsodyks, Head of the
Computational Neurobiology Program at The Weizmann Institute (see
attached). He is interested in participating in the project. If we are
able to bring him and his associates into the picture, we would create a
much more powerful team than most such groups found in the USA. Ideally
then we would have a combined US-Israeli team. We would conceptualize
things together; do modeling work in Israel; and experimental work in
New York. This could be a very compelling project on many levels. I hope
that the concept appeals to you and look forward to your response.

Best wishes,


Nick

Elkhonon Goldberg, Ph.D., ABPP

Clinical Professor of Neurology
New York University School of Medicine

Diplomate
American Board of Professional Psychology
in Clinical Neuropsychology

315 West 57th Street
Suite 401
New York, NY 10019
USA

tel +1 212 541 6412
fax +1 212 765 7158
egneurocog@aol.com
elkhonon.goldberg@med.nyu.edu
www.elkhonongoldberg.com









________________________________

From: egneurocog@aol.com [mailto:egneurocog@aol.com]
Sent: Monday, March 17, 2008 7:46 PM
To: Lesley Groff; Lesley Groff
Subject: WEIZMANN INSTITUTE

 

Dear Jeffrey,

 

I've given further thought to our discussion last week and to your suggestion that people with more basic mathematical, as opposed to engineering, background be brought into the project. Instead of thinking in terms of individuals, as was my initial inclination, I approached the issue more systemically. In my opinion, the absolutely best neural-net modelling, and generally mathematical neurobiology talent is found in Israel. Over the last few days I had an email exchange, and yesterday a long telephone conversation with Prof. Misha Tsodyks, Head of the Computational Neurobiology Program at The Weizmann Institute (see attached). He is interested in participating in the project. If we are able to bring him and his associates into the picture, we would create a much more powerful team than most such groups found in the USA. Ideally then we would have a combined US-Israeli team. We would conceptualize things together; do modeling work in Israel; and experimental work in New York. This could be a very compelling project on many levels. I hope that the concept appeals to you and look forward to your response.

Best wishes,


Nick
